meta.report_title: Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) — Space Launch & Systems

scqa.situation: Rocket Lab USA is the world's second-most-launched orbital rocket company (after SpaceX), operating the Electron small-lift rocket (17 successful launches in 2025 totaling $87M launch revenue) and growing a Space Systems segment (satellites, spacecraft components). Q1 2026 revenue was $200.3M, up 67% YoY, with Space Systems contributing $165.4M (83% of revenue) driven by constellation satellite contract delivery. The company's $2.2B backlog (as of May 2026) covers approximately 2.8 years of current run-rate revenue.
scqa.complication: Rocket Lab is not yet profitable: Q1 2026 GAAP net loss was $45M, with non-GAAP operating loss of $12M. The Neutron medium-lift rocket (targeting 13-ton LEO payload) is in development for a first launch target of 2026, requiring significant capital ($100M+ estimated remaining development cost). The company faces direct competition from SpaceX Falcon 9 for Neutron's target market and from Firefly/ABL for Electron's small-sat market.
scqa.question: Can Rocket Lab's Space Systems scale and Neutron commercialization justify a US$10.8B market cap on $800M annual revenue run-rate, before achieving GAAP profitability?
scqa.answer: The thesis depends on Neutron: if Neutron achieves first commercial flight by H2 2026 and wins 5+ contract launches by 2027, RKLB's valuation is defensible on a EV/Revenue basis (13x NTM vs SpaceX implied 40x+ on secondary market). Space Systems $2.2B backlog provides visibility. Near-term downside risk: Neutron delay beyond Q4 2026 could trigger 20-30% multiple compression. Base 12-month price target: US$26.

key_points: Q1 2026 revenue $200.3M (+67% YoY); Space Systems $165.4M (83% of revenue); Launch Services $34.9M (17%)
Backlog $2.2B as of May 7, 2026 — includes SDA (Space Development Agency) satellite contracts and undisclosed government/commercial spacecraft awards
Gross margin 38.2% (non-GAAP Q1 2026), up from 29.4% in Q1 2025 — mix shift to higher-margin satellite programs
Neutron development on track: first launch target H2 2026; Stage 1 Archimedes engine tests complete; Wallops Island launch complex construction active

company_data.description: Rocket Lab USA is an end-to-end space company providing launch services and space systems. The Electron rocket (17 launches in 2025) serves commercial and government smallsat customers with a $7.5M average launch price. The Neutron rocket (in development) targets medium-lift LEO/GEO commercial and national security payloads. The Space Systems segment designs and manufactures spacecraft, satellite buses, and components (solar panels, reaction wheels, star trackers) for government and commercial customers including NASA, DARPA, and BlackSky.

segments.name: Space Systems
segments.description: Satellite manufacturing, spacecraft buses, components (solar panels, reaction wheels, propulsion). Q1 2026: $165.4M. Driven by SDA tranche satellite deliveries

segments.name: Launch Services
segments.description: Electron small-lift rocket launches (17 in 2025, target 22+ in 2026); $7.5M average per launch. Q1 2026: $34.9M (3 launches in Q1)

balance_sheet.source: Rocket Lab Form 10-Q filed 2026-05-09, Balance Sheet as of March 31, 2026

financials.source: Rocket Lab USA Form 10-Q filed 2026-05-09 (SEC EDGAR CIK 0001819994, period ended 2026-03-31) and 8-K earnings release filed 2026-05-07

management_quotes.speaker: Peter Beck, Founder & CEO
management_quotes.quote: Rocket Lab had a tremendous Q1 with revenue of $200 million, a 67% year-over-year increase. Our backlog stands at $2.2 billion and we're seeing strong demand across both our launch and space systems businesses. Neutron development is progressing well and we remain on track for our first launch in the back half of this year.
management_quotes.source: Rocket Lab USA 8-K Exhibit 99.1 (earnings release), filed 2026-05-07, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001819994

management_quotes.speaker: Adam Spice, CFO
management_quotes.quote: We ended Q1 with $515 million in cash and short-term investments. Gross margin on a non-GAAP basis was 38.2%, reflecting the favorable mix from our Space Systems segment. We expect Q2 revenue of $215-225 million with continued gross margin strength.
management_quotes.source: Rocket Lab USA 8-K Exhibit 99.1 (earnings release), filed 2026-05-07, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001819994

product_deep_dive.why_key: Electron is the world's most commercially successful dedicated small launch vehicle, with 17 launches in 2025 and a 20+ launch cadence target for 2026. It is the near-term cash generator and operational proof-point. Neutron is the medium-lift successor targeting the $1B+ annual government and commercial launch market currently served by Falcon 9. Neutron is the primary long-term value driver.

specs.detail: 13,000kg to LEO; 1,500kg to GTO (reusable, Stage 1 return)
Archimedes engine tests complete; Wallops launch complex under construction; first launch target H2 2026

product_deep_dive.market_size: Small launch market: ~$1.5B TAM (2025), CAGR 15%. Medium launch market: ~$3B TAM (2025), growing to $8B by 2030 as Neutron targets Falcon 9 replacement. Space Systems components and satellite manufacturing: ~$25B TAM by 2030.
product_deep_dive.revenue_contribution: Space Systems $165.4M (83%) + Launch Services $34.9M (17%) in Q1 2026. Space Systems driven primarily by SDA (Space Development Agency) Tranche 2 satellite delivery contracts worth $150M+ total

positive.label: Neutron reusability IP (Archimedes engine, carbon fiber tank)

negative.label: Neutron development risk (first launch target H2 2026)

technical.volume_trend: Q1 法說後連續放量，顯示動能延續
technical.note: 強勢偏多格局，但 RSI 接近 70 短期注意獲利了結風險

cashflow.title: 現金流分析（Q1 2026）
cashflow.lead: Q1 2026 現金消耗 $85M（含 Neutron 開發投入）。現金餘額 $515M，含可轉換票據 $355M（2029 年到期）。

ocf.label: OCF Q1 2026
ocf.note: 含 SBC + 積壓合約預付款變動；Source: 10-Q 2026-05-09

cfi.label: Capex Q1 2026
cfi.note: 主要為 Neutron 發射台 Wallops 建設 + Electron 設備

cff.label: 融資 Q1 2026
cff.note: 無新增融資；可轉換票據 $355M 2029 年到期

cashflow.fcf_note: Neutron 發射台建設為 FY2026 最大資本支出項目，預計完成後 FCF 改善。$515M 現金覆蓋 ~2 年當前燒錢速率。
cashflow.runway_conclusion: 以 Q1 2026 消耗率（$85M/季）計算，$515M 現金提供約 6 季跑道，足夠支撐 Neutron 首發及後續量產準備。若 Neutron 2027 年開始商業飛行，OCF 轉正可期。

bear.label: 保守
bear.assumption: Neutron delayed to 2028; Space Systems growth decelerates to 20% YoY in 2027; market de-rates to 8x NTM Revenue; EV ~$4.5B → PT $9-12

base.label: 基本
base.assumption: Neutron first commercial flight H1 2027; 3 Neutron launches in 2027 at $50M each; Space Systems $1.2B revenue by 2027; EV/Revenue 12x → PT $26

bull.label: 樂觀
bull.assumption: Neutron exceeds schedule; 10+ Neutron launches in 2027; DARPA/DoD anchor contracts; market re-rates to 20x Revenue; 2027 Revenue $1.8B → PT $45

risks.title: Neutron 開發進度風險
risks.detail: 首飛目標 H2 2026 若延至 2027 或更晚，觸發估值重評壓縮 20-30%。Neutron 開發成本超支亦將加速現金消耗，嚴重性：高

risks.title: SpaceX 競爭（Falcon 9）
risks.detail: SpaceX Falcon 9 已有 80+ 次再利用紀錄，每次飛行成本 $30M-40M，遠低於 Neutron 初期定價。Neutron 需在可靠性和成本取得可信度，嚴重性：高

risks.title: GAAP 虧損及稀釋風險
risks.detail: Q1 2026 GAAP 淨損 $45M；股票酬勞每季 ~$35M 持續稀釋。若無法在 2027 年達到 EBITDA 轉正，估值支撐將受考驗，嚴重性：中

risks.title: 政策/監管風險
risks.detail: 美國 FCC/FAA 發射許可若延誤，電子火箭頻率可能受限。若美中關係惡化，衛星出口管制可能影響 Space Systems 合約，嚴重性：中

risks.title: 現金流不足風險
risks.detail: $515M 現金在 $85M/季消耗率下提供 ~6 季跑道。若 Neutron 2027 年才首飛且拿不到足夠預付款，可能需要額外融資（稀釋）

catalysts.title: Neutron 首次飛行（目標 H2 2026）
catalysts.detail: 最大估值催化劑。成功首飛可觸發市場對 RKLB 從小型火箭公司到中型火箭的重新定價

catalysts.title: SDA Tranche 2 衛星交付完成（H2 2026）
catalysts.detail: 確認 Space Systems 執行能力，帶動 Q3/Q4 2026 收入高度能見度

catalysts.title: Neutron 商業合約首筆簽署（2026/2027）
catalysts.detail: 政府或商業 Neutron 錨定客戶合約，消除市場對 Neutron 商業可行性疑慮

catalysts.title: Electron 月發射頻率突破 2 次（2026）
catalysts.detail: Launch Services 季度收入突破 $50M，小型火箭業務獨立商業化能力確認

supply_chain.position: Vertically Integrated Launch Provider + Satellite Manufacturer
supply_chain.description: Rocket Lab manufactures 90%+ of Electron components in-house including carbon composite tanks, Rutherford engines (3D printed), avionics, and software. Post-acquisitions (Sinclair Interplanetary, Advanced Solutions Inc., SolAero, Planetary Systems Corp.), Rocket Lab now produces solar panels, reaction wheels, star trackers, propulsion systems, and spacecraft buses internally, enabling full constellation delivery from components to deployment.

industry.name: Commercial Space Launch + Satellite Manufacturing
drivers: Commercial LEO broadband (Starlink, OneWeb expansion driving component demand)
Neutron addressable market: $3B+ annual medium-lift demand by 2028
industry.competitive_dynamics: Launch market: SpaceX dominates with ~75% market share. Rocket Lab is #2 with 17% of orbital launches ex-SpaceX. Satellite manufacturing: fragmented, Rocket Lab differentiates via vertical integration and government cleared status.

bull_reasons: 全球軌道火箭發射頻率 #2（SpaceX 後），Electron 商業化已成熟
訂單積壓 $2.2B 提供約 2.8 年能見度，Space Systems 執行穩定
Neutron 若成功量產，TAM 從 $1.5B 擴張至 $12B+
垂直整合衛星製造（太陽能板、反應輪、星追儀）提供差異化和更高毛利率
bear_reasons: 市值 $10.8B 對應 $800M 年化營收，EV/Revenue 13x 含大量 Neutron 期權溢價
SpaceX Falcon 9 可靠性和成本優勢短期難以匹敵
GAAP 虧損持續，Q1 2026 淨損 $45M，盈利轉正時程不確定
現金跑道 ~6 季，若 Neutron 延誤可能需要再融資
key_monitor: Neutron 首次飛行時間（最關鍵里程碑）
Electron 年度發射次數（目標 22+ 次，2026）
季度現金消耗率（目標維持 $70-90M/季）
Space Systems 新合約簽約金額（積壓補充速度）
Neutron 商業合約首批錨定客戶確認

disclaimer: 本報告由 Investment Research Desk 提供，僅供資訊參考，不構成投資建議或要約。所有預估數據均為分析判斷，不保證準確性。投資決策前請諮詢持牌財務顧問並自行評估風險。本報告不得作為任何投資決策之唯一依據。過去表現不代表未來結果。

earnings.title: 最新法說重點
earnings.lead: Q1 2026 法說會：收入 $200M（+67% YoY）創歷史新高；積壓 $2.2B；Neutron 首飛仍在 H2 2026 目標

quotes.text: Rocket Lab had a tremendous Q1 with revenue of $200 million, a 67% year-over-year increase. Our backlog stands at $2.2 billion and we're seeing strong demand across both our launch and space systems businesses. Neutron development is progressing well and we remain on track for our first launch in the back half of this year.
quotes.attr: Peter Beck, Founder & CEO — Rocket Lab USA 8-K Exhibit 99.1, 2026-05-07

quotes.text: We ended Q1 with $515 million in cash and short-term investments. Gross margin on a non-GAAP basis was 38.2%, reflecting the favorable mix from our Space Systems segment. We expect Q2 revenue of $215-225 million with continued gross margin strength.
quotes.attr: Adam Spice, CFO — Rocket Lab USA 8-K Exhibit 99.1, 2026-05-07

qa.question: Neutron 首飛時程有無風險？
qa.response: H2 2026 維持，Archimedes 發動機測試完成

qa.question: Space Systems 積壓組成？
qa.response: 主要為 SDA Tranche 2 + 未披露政府合約

qa.question: FCF 轉正時程？
qa.response: Neutron capex 高峰 2026，2027 後 FCF 改善

earnings.market_reaction: 法說後股價+8.3%（次日），Space Systems 加速為主要正面驚喜
